{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=draft}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|12=draft}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|ja|貞享暦|12=draft}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Senmyō calendar|ja|宣明暦|12=draft}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha Bugyō|ja|寺社奉行|12=draft}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=draft}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|ja|若年寄|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=draft}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=draft}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|ja|西川如見|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|ja|高橋至時|12=draft}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=draft}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=draft}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Institute for Western Studies|ja|蕃書調所|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|The University of Tokyo|ja|東京大学|12=draft}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=draft}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|ja|寛保|12=draft}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=draft}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=draft}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=draft}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=draft}}. It moved to {{ill|Kanda Surugadai|ja|神田駿河台|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=draft}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=draft}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|ja|伊能忠敬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=draft}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|ja|国立天文台|12=draft}} {{div col end}}

By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=draft}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=draft}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Institute for Western Studies|ja|蕃書調所|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|The University of Tokyo|ja|東京大学|12=draft}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=draft}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|ja|寛保|12=draft}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=draft}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=draft}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=draft}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=draft}}. It moved to {{ill|Kanda Surugadai|ja|神田駿河台|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=draft}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=draft}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|ja|伊能忠敬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=draft}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|ja|国立天文台|12=draft}} {{div col end}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|陰陽寮|" to "|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|"
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=draft}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|12=draft}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|ja|貞享暦|12=draft}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Senmyō calendar|ja|宣明暦|12=draft}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha Bugyō|ja|寺社奉行|12=draft}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=draft}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|ja|若年寄|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=draft}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=draft}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|ja|西川如見|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|ja|高橋至時|12=draft}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=draft}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=draft}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Institute for Western Studies|ja|蕃書調所|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|The University of Tokyo|ja|東京大学|12=draft}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=draft}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|ja|寛保|12=draft}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=draft}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=draft}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=draft}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=draft}}. It moved to {{ill|Kanda Surugadai|ja|神田駿河台|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=draft}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=draft}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|ja|伊能忠敬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=draft}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|ja|国立天文台|12=draft}} {{div col end}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|Tenmonkata|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)}} <!--Tenmongata-->

Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|ja|若年寄|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=draft}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=draft}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|ja|西川如見|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|ja|高橋至時|12=draft}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=draft}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=draft}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Institute for Western Studies|ja|蕃書調所|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|The University of Tokyo|ja|東京大学|12=draft}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=draft}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|ja|寛保|12=draft}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=draft}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=draft}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=draft}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=draft}}. It moved to {{ill|Kanda Surugadai|ja|神田駿河台|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=draft}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=draft}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|ja|伊能忠敬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=draft}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|ja|国立天文台|12=draft}} {{div col end}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|Tenmonkata|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)}} <!--Tenmongata-->

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=draft}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|12=draft}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|ja|貞享暦|12=draft}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Senmyō calendar|ja|宣明暦|12=draft}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha Bugyō|ja|寺社奉行|12=draft}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=draft}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|ja|若年寄|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=draft}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=draft}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|ja|西川如見|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|ja|高橋至時|12=draft}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|12=draft}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=draft}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=draft}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Institute for Western Studies|ja|蕃書調所|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ansei|ja|安政|12=draft}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|The University of Tokyo|ja|東京大学|12=draft}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|ja|渋川春海|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=draft}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|ja|寛保|12=draft}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=draft}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Sukezumi|ja|山路主住|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=draft}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=draft}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=draft}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|12=draft}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=draft}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=draft}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=draft}}. It moved to {{ill|Kanda Surugadai|ja|神田駿河台|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|ja|浅草|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=draft}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=draft}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|ja|伊能忠敬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=draft}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|ja|国立天文台|12=draft}} {{div col end}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年7月}} '''天文方'''（てんもんかた）は、[[江戸幕府]]によって設置された天体運行および暦の研究機関。主に[[編暦]]を司った。 == 概要 == 元々、編暦作業は[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]の[[陰陽寮]]の所轄であり、[[土御門家]]があたっていたが、[[貞享]]元年（[[1684年]]）に[[渋川春海]]が[[貞享暦]]を作成し、従来の[[宣明暦]]から切り替えると、幕府は[[寺社奉行]]のもとに天文方を設置し、同年[[12月1日 (旧暦)|12月1日]]（[[1685年]][[1月5日]]）に春海が天文方（役職名としては「天文職」とも）に就任した。以来、編暦作業の実務は幕府に移り、天文方で行われた。初めは[[寺社奉行]]の下に位置したが、[[延享]]4年[[1月23日 (旧暦)|1月23日]]（[[1747年]][[3月4日]]）に[[若年寄]]支配となっている。[[俸禄]]は100俵で、他に[[役料]]として5ないし10人扶持が加算された。 天文方は世襲制であったが、時には[[天文学]]に通じた人物を追加あるいは[[養子縁組]]して世襲を許したために、幕末までに渋川家、[[猪飼家]]、西川家、山路家、吉田家、奥村家、高橋家、足立家の8家が任命され、状況により優秀な人材が登用された。もっとも家系が断絶した家もあり、[[西川如見]]（子の[[西川正休|正休]]が[[徳川吉宗]]によって天文方に招聘）や[[高橋至時]]の子孫も[[幕末]]までは継承されず、最終的には渋川家・山路家・足立家のみが存続した。 [[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年（[[1811年]]）に[[高橋景保]]の提案によって、外局として[[蛮書和解御用]]が設置され、[[安政]]3年（[[1858年]]）の[[蕃書調所]]設置まで続いている。幕末には編暦以外にも[[天文]]{{要曖昧さ回避|date=2023年5月}}や測量、地誌、洋書翻訳なども取り仕切った。[[東京大学]]の起源の一つである。 == 天文方の各家 == ;渋川家 :渋川春海の改暦の功績により、貞享元年（1684年）天文方となる。養子縁組を繰り返しながらも幕末まで継承された。 :[[渋川春海]]－[[渋川昔尹|昔尹]]－[[渋川敬尹|敬尹]]－[[渋川敬也|敬也]]－[[渋川則休|則休]]－[[渋川光洪|光洪]]－[[渋川正清|正清]]－[[渋川正陽|正陽]]－[[渋川景佑|景佑]]－（[[渋川敬直|敬直]]）－[[渋川佑賢|佑賢]]－[[渋川敬典|敬典]] ;猪飼家 :御徒であった[[猪飼正一]]（豊次郎）が[[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）[[渋川敬尹]]の暦作御用手伝となり、[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）天文方になる。[[寛保]]元年（[[1741年]]）に正一が没すると後継者なく、1代限りとなる。 ;西川家 :長崎の天文家である西川如見の息子の西川正休が延享4年（1747年）天文方となり、2代続く。 :（[[西川如見]]）－[[西川正休|正休]]－[[西川忠喬|忠喬]] ;山路家 :[[山路主住]]が宝暦の改暦（[[宝暦暦]]）の際に[[渋川則休]]と西川正休の補暦御用手伝となり、[[明和]]元年（[[1764年]]）に天文方に任命されたのに始まる。2代[[山路之徽|之徽]]は天文方に任命されなかったが、3代[[山路徳風|徳風]]以後、幕末まで天文方を継承した。なお、徳風の玄孫[[山路愛山|愛山]]は、作家として知られている。 :山路主住－（[[山路之徽|之徽]]）－[[山路徳風|徳風]]－[[山路諧孝|諧孝]]－[[山路彰常|彰常]]－（[[山路彰善|彰善]]）－（[[山路愛山|愛山]]） ;吉田家 :佐々木長秀（後に[[吉田秀長]]）が宝暦の改暦（宝暦暦）の際に西川正休の息子[[西川忠喬|忠喬]]の作暦手伝となり、明和元年（1764年）天文方に任じられ宝暦暦修正事業を命じられた。以後、吉田家は幕末まで天文方を継承した。 :吉田秀長－[[吉田秀升|秀升]]－[[吉田秀賢|秀賢]]－[[吉田秀茂|秀茂]] ;奥村家 :[[奥村邦俊]]が明和2年（[[1765年]]）に新暦調手伝となり、[[天明]]7年（[[1787年]]）天文方に任じられた。1代限りである。 ;高橋家 :高橋至時が[[寛政]]7年（[[1795年]]）に天文方に任命されたのに始まる。至時の長男である[[高橋景保|景保]]が[[シーボルト事件]]に関与して獄死したため、高橋家は2代で終わるが、次男の[[渋川景佑|景佑]]が渋川家の養子となっている。 :[[高橋至時]]－[[高橋景保|景保]] ;足立家 :[[足立信頭]]が寛政の改暦（[[寛政暦]]）のために高橋至時の助手となり、[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）天文方に任じられた。幕末まで2代にわたり天文方を務めた。 :[[足立信頭]]－（[[足立信順|信順]]）－[[足立信行|信行]] == 天文方の観測施設 == [[File:Hokusai, Fuji at Torigoe.jpg|thumb|right|270px|[[葛飾北斎]]画：『[[鳥越の不二]]』
浅草天文台にて]] 渋川春海が天文方に任じられた翌貞享2年（[[1685年]]）に[[牛込]]藁町の地に'''司天台'''を設置した。[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に[[本所 (墨田区)|本所]]、同14年（[[1701年]]）に[[神田駿河台]]に移転する。春海の没後、[[延享]]3年（[[1746年]]）{{要出典|date=2021年12月}}に[[神田佐久間町]]、[[明和]]2年（1765年）に牛込袋町に移り、[[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]）に[[浅草]]の'''浅草天文台'''（'''頒暦所'''とも）に移った。この時に「天文台」という呼称が初めて採用された。高橋至時や[[間重富]]が寛政の改暦に従事したのは牛込袋町・浅草時代であり、[[伊能忠敬]]が高橋至時の元で天文学・[[測量学]]を学んだのも浅草天文台であった。その後、天保13年（[[1842年]]）に渋川景佑らの尽力で[[九段坂|九段坂上]]にもう1つの天文台が設置されて天体観測に従事した。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に天文方とともに浅草・九段の両天文台が廃止される事になる。 == 天文方の廃止 == 大政奉還、[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]後、[[土御門晴雄]]は朝廷に願い出て、暦算、[[頒暦]]の権限を土御門家に取り戻し、天文方は廃止されて、同職にあった[[渋川敬典]]・[[山路彰常]]に代わって、晴雄が陰陽頭として責任者の地位に就いた。土御門家は明治2年の作歴を行うが、同年晴雄が没し、土御門家を11歳の子・和丸（[[土御門晴栄]]）が継ぐ事となり、明治3年（[[1870年]]）、天文暦道の権限は文部省の天文暦道局に移され、同年旧暦8月7日天文暦道局は東京に移され、旧暦8月25日星学局と改称された。星学局には旧天文方の渋川敬典も任用された。同年末、土御門晴栄は大学御用係を免職となり、暦道における土御門家の特権は廃止された。 == 関連項目 == {{Div col}} *[[伊能忠敬]] *[[入間川重恒]] *[[国立天文台]] *[[箕作秋坪]] *[[山路主住]] {{Div col end}} == 外部リンク == *{{Kotobank|天文方|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)}} {{東京大学の源流・前身諸機関}} {{Astro-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんもんかた}} [[Category:天文学に関する記事]] [[Category:日本の天文学史]] [[Category:江戸幕府の学校・研究機関]] [[Category:江戸幕府の職制]] [[Category:江戸時代の天文学者|*]] [[Category:17世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:18世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:19世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:江戸時代の天文学]] [[Category:暦法]] [[Category:開成所]] [[Category:渋川家|*]] [[Category:山路家|*]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[天文方]] to [[Tenmongata]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年7月}} '''天文方'''（てんもんかた）は、[[江戸幕府]]によって設置された天体運行および暦の研究機関。主に[[編暦]]を司った。 == 概要 == 元々、編暦作業は[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]の[[陰陽寮]]の所轄であり、[[土御門家]]があたっていたが、[[貞享]]元年（[[1684年]]）に[[渋川春海]]が[[貞享暦]]を作成し、従来の[[宣明暦]]から切り替えると、幕府は[[寺社奉行]]のもとに天文方を設置し、同年[[12月1日 (旧暦)|12月1日]]（[[1685年]][[1月5日]]）に春海が天文方（役職名としては「天文職」とも）に就任した。以来、編暦作業の実務は幕府に移り、天文方で行われた。初めは[[寺社奉行]]の下に位置したが、[[延享]]4年[[1月23日 (旧暦)|1月23日]]（[[1747年]][[3月4日]]）に[[若年寄]]支配となっている。[[俸禄]]は100俵で、他に[[役料]]として5ないし10人扶持が加算された。 天文方は世襲制であったが、時には[[天文学]]に通じた人物を追加あるいは[[養子縁組]]して世襲を許したために、幕末までに渋川家、[[猪飼家]]、西川家、山路家、吉田家、奥村家、高橋家、足立家の8家が任命され、状況により優秀な人材が登用された。もっとも家系が断絶した家もあり、[[西川如見]]（子の[[西川正休|正休]]が[[徳川吉宗]]によって天文方に招聘）や[[高橋至時]]の子孫も[[幕末]]までは継承されず、最終的には渋川家・山路家・足立家のみが存続した。 [[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年（[[1811年]]）に[[高橋景保]]の提案によって、外局として[[蛮書和解御用]]が設置され、[[安政]]3年（[[1858年]]）の[[蕃書調所]]設置まで続いている。幕末には編暦以外にも[[天文]]{{要曖昧さ回避|date=2023年5月}}や測量、地誌、洋書翻訳なども取り仕切った。[[東京大学]]の起源の一つである。 == 天文方の各家 == ;渋川家 :渋川春海の改暦の功績により、貞享元年（1684年）天文方となる。養子縁組を繰り返しながらも幕末まで継承された。 :[[渋川春海]]－[[渋川昔尹|昔尹]]－[[渋川敬尹|敬尹]]－[[渋川敬也|敬也]]－[[渋川則休|則休]]－[[渋川光洪|光洪]]－[[渋川正清|正清]]－[[渋川正陽|正陽]]－[[渋川景佑|景佑]]－（[[渋川敬直|敬直]]）－[[渋川佑賢|佑賢]]－[[渋川敬典|敬典]] ;猪飼家 :御徒であった[[猪飼正一]]（豊次郎）が[[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）[[渋川敬尹]]の暦作御用手伝となり、[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）天文方になる。[[寛保]]元年（[[1741年]]）に正一が没すると後継者なく、1代限りとなる。 ;西川家 :長崎の天文家である西川如見の息子の西川正休が延享4年（1747年）天文方となり、2代続く。 :（[[西川如見]]）－[[西川正休|正休]]－[[西川忠喬|忠喬]] ;山路家 :[[山路主住]]が宝暦の改暦（[[宝暦暦]]）の際に[[渋川則休]]と西川正休の補暦御用手伝となり、[[明和]]元年（[[1764年]]）に天文方に任命されたのに始まる。2代[[山路之徽|之徽]]は天文方に任命されなかったが、3代[[山路徳風|徳風]]以後、幕末まで天文方を継承した。なお、徳風の玄孫[[山路愛山|愛山]]は、作家として知られている。 :山路主住－（[[山路之徽|之徽]]）－[[山路徳風|徳風]]－[[山路諧孝|諧孝]]－[[山路彰常|彰常]]－（[[山路彰善|彰善]]）－（[[山路愛山|愛山]]） ;吉田家 :佐々木長秀（後に[[吉田秀長]]）が宝暦の改暦（宝暦暦）の際に西川正休の息子[[西川忠喬|忠喬]]の作暦手伝となり、明和元年（1764年）天文方に任じられ宝暦暦修正事業を命じられた。以後、吉田家は幕末まで天文方を継承した。 :吉田秀長－[[吉田秀升|秀升]]－[[吉田秀賢|秀賢]]－[[吉田秀茂|秀茂]] ;奥村家 :[[奥村邦俊]]が明和2年（[[1765年]]）に新暦調手伝となり、[[天明]]7年（[[1787年]]）天文方に任じられた。1代限りである。 ;高橋家 :高橋至時が[[寛政]]7年（[[1795年]]）に天文方に任命されたのに始まる。至時の長男である[[高橋景保|景保]]が[[シーボルト事件]]に関与して獄死したため、高橋家は2代で終わるが、次男の[[渋川景佑|景佑]]が渋川家の養子となっている。 :[[高橋至時]]－[[高橋景保|景保]] ;足立家 :[[足立信頭]]が寛政の改暦（[[寛政暦]]）のために高橋至時の助手となり、[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）天文方に任じられた。幕末まで2代にわたり天文方を務めた。 :[[足立信頭]]－（[[足立信順|信順]]）－[[足立信行|信行]] == 天文方の観測施設 == [[File:Hokusai, Fuji at Torigoe.jpg|thumb|right|270px|[[葛飾北斎]]画：『[[鳥越の不二]]』
浅草天文台にて]] 渋川春海が天文方に任じられた翌貞享2年（[[1685年]]）に[[牛込]]藁町の地に'''司天台'''を設置した。[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に[[本所 (墨田区)|本所]]、同14年（[[1701年]]）に[[神田駿河台]]に移転する。春海の没後、[[延享]]3年（[[1746年]]）{{要出典|date=2021年12月}}に[[神田佐久間町]]、[[明和]]2年（1765年）に牛込袋町に移り、[[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]）に[[浅草]]の'''浅草天文台'''（'''頒暦所'''とも）に移った。この時に「天文台」という呼称が初めて採用された。高橋至時や[[間重富]]が寛政の改暦に従事したのは牛込袋町・浅草時代であり、[[伊能忠敬]]が高橋至時の元で天文学・[[測量学]]を学んだのも浅草天文台であった。その後、天保13年（[[1842年]]）に渋川景佑らの尽力で[[九段坂|九段坂上]]にもう1つの天文台が設置されて天体観測に従事した。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に天文方とともに浅草・九段の両天文台が廃止される事になる。 == 天文方の廃止 == 大政奉還、[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]後、[[土御門晴雄]]は朝廷に願い出て、暦算、[[頒暦]]の権限を土御門家に取り戻し、天文方は廃止されて、同職にあった[[渋川敬典]]・[[山路彰常]]に代わって、晴雄が陰陽頭として責任者の地位に就いた。土御門家は明治2年の作歴を行うが、同年晴雄が没し、土御門家を11歳の子・和丸（[[土御門晴栄]]）が継ぐ事となり、明治3年（[[1870年]]）、天文暦道の権限は文部省の天文暦道局に移され、同年旧暦8月7日天文暦道局は東京に移され、旧暦8月25日星学局と改称された。星学局には旧天文方の渋川敬典も任用された。同年末、土御門晴栄は大学御用係を免職となり、暦道における土御門家の特権は廃止された。 == 関連項目 == {{Div col}} *[[伊能忠敬]] *[[入間川重恒]] *[[国立天文台]] *[[箕作秋坪]] *[[山路主住]] {{Div col end}} == 外部リンク == *{{Kotobank|天文方|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)}} {{東京大学の源流・前身諸機関}} {{Astro-stub}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんもんかた}} [[Category:天文学に関する記事]] [[Category:日本の天文学史]] [[Category:江戸幕府の学校・研究機関]] [[Category:江戸幕府の職制]] [[Category:江戸時代の天文学者|*]] [[Category:17世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:18世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:19世紀日本の天文学者|*]] [[Category:江戸時代の天文学]] [[Category:暦法]] [[Category:開成所]] [[Category:渋川家|*]] [[Category:山路家|*]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684), [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] created the [[:en:Jōkyō calendar|Jōkyō calendar]], replacing the traditional [[:en:Xuanming calendar|Senmyō calendar]]. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Jisha Bugyō]]. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the [[:en:Wakadoshiyori|Wakadoshiyori]] on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|qq=|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of [[:en:Nishikawa Joken|Nishikawa Joken]] and [[:en:Takahashi Yoshitoki|Takahashi Yoshitoki]], did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|qq=|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|qq=|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the [[:en:Bansho Shirabesho|Institute for Western Studies]] in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of [[:en:University of Tokyo|The University of Tokyo]].
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|qq=|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|qq=|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561574}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 入間川重恒|1=Irumagawa Shigetsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389078}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Noriyasu|qq=|1=Shibukawa Noriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561560}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|qq=|1=Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561556}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masakiyo|qq=|1=Shibukawa Masakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561581}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|qq=|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|qq=|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keichoku|qq=|1=Shibukawa Hironao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561576}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|qq=|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|qq=|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716). After his death in [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō]] 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|qq=|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|qq=|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|qq=|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|qq=|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|qq=|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to [[:en:Surugadai|Kanda Surugadai]] in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) and later to [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]]. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--> [[Category:17th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:18th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:Articles about astronomy]] [[Category:Yamaji Clan]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Schools and Research Institutions of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Administrative System of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Astronomy in the Edo period]] [[Category:Astronomers of the Edo period]] [[Category:Shibukawa family]] [[Category:Kaiseijo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:천문방]] [[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]] [[zh:天文方]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:천문방]] [[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]] [[zh:天文方]] {{wikidata link|Q11442660}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684), [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] created the [[:en:Jōkyō calendar|Jōkyō calendar]], replacing the traditional [[:en:Xuanming calendar|Senmyō calendar]]. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Jisha Bugyō]]. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the [[:en:Wakadoshiyori|Wakadoshiyori]] on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|qq=|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of [[:en:Nishikawa Joken|Nishikawa Joken]] and [[:en:Takahashi Yoshitoki|Takahashi Yoshitoki]], did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|qq=|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|qq=|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the [[:en:Bansho Shirabesho|Institute for Western Studies]] in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of [[:en:University of Tokyo|The University of Tokyo]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684), [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] created the [[:en:Jōkyō calendar|Jōkyō calendar]], replacing the traditional [[:en:Xuanming calendar|Senmyō calendar]]. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Jisha Bugyō]]. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the [[:en:Wakadoshiyori|Wakadoshiyori]] on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of [[:en:Nishikawa Joken|Nishikawa Joken]] and [[:en:Takahashi Yoshitoki|Takahashi Yoshitoki]], did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the [[:en:Bansho Shirabesho|Institute for Western Studies]] in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of [[:en:University of Tokyo|The University of Tokyo]].
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716). After his death in [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō]] 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to [[:en:Surugadai|Kanda Surugadai]] in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) and later to [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]]. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684), [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] created the [[:en:Jōkyō calendar|Jōkyō calendar]], replacing the traditional [[:en:Xuanming calendar|Senmyō calendar]]. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Jisha Bugyō]]. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the [[:en:Wakadoshiyori|Wakadoshiyori]] on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of [[:en:Nishikawa Joken|Nishikawa Joken]] and [[:en:Takahashi Yoshitoki|Takahashi Yoshitoki]], did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the [[:en:Bansho Shirabesho|Institute for Western Studies]] in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of [[:en:University of Tokyo|The University of Tokyo]].

; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716). After his death in [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō]] 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to [[:en:Surugadai|Kanda Surugadai]] in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) and later to [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]]. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * [[:en:Inō Tadataka|Inō Tadataka]] * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsukuri Shūkichi|1=Mitsukuri Shūhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532071}} * [[:en:National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan]] {{div col end}}

<!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--><!--Tenmongata--> [[Category:17th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:18th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:Articles about astronomy]] [[Category:Yamaji Clan]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Schools and Research Institutions of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Administrative System of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Astronomy in the Edo period]] [[Category:Astronomers of the Edo period]] [[Category:Shibukawa family]] [[Category:Kaiseijo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:천문방]] [[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]] [[zh:天文方]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:천문방]] [[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]] [[zh:天文方]] {{wikidata link|Q11442660}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684), [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] created the [[:en:Jōkyō calendar|Jōkyō calendar]], replacing the traditional [[:en:Xuanming calendar|Senmyō calendar]]. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the [[:en:Jisha-bugyō|Jisha Bugyō]]. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the [[:en:Wakadoshiyori|Wakadoshiyori]] on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of [[:en:Nishikawa Joken|Nishikawa Joken]] and [[:en:Takahashi Yoshitoki|Takahashi Yoshitoki]], did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the [[:en:Bansho Shirabesho|Institute for Western Studies]] in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of [[:en:University of Tokyo|The University of Tokyo]].
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : [[:en:Shibukawa Shunkai|Shibukawa Shunkai]] – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561574}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 入間川重恒|1=Irumagawa Shigetsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389078}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Noriyasu|1=Shibukawa Noriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561560}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|1=Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561556}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masakiyo|1=Shibukawa Masakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561581}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keichoku|1=Shibukawa Hironao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561576}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716). After his death in [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō]] 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to [[:en:Surugadai|Kanda Surugadai]] in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) and later to [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]]. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
