Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716). After his death in [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō]] 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : [[:en:Yamaji Nushizumi|Yamaji Sukezumi]] – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to [[:en:Surugadai|Kanda Surugadai]] in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) and later to [[:en:Asakusa|Asakusa]]. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|1=Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561556}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keichoku|1=Shibukawa Hironao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561576}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo}}.

After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.

The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|en|Inō Tadataka|WD=Q1240937|lt=Inō Tadataka}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsukuri Shūkichi|1=Mitsukuri Shūhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532071}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|en|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|WD=Q1327713|lt=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}} {{div col end}}

{{wikidata link|Q11442660}} [[Category:17th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:18th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese astronomers]] [[Category:Administrative System of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Articles about astronomy]] [[Category:Astronomers of the Edo period]] [[Category:Astronomy in the Edo period]] [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Kaiseijo]] [[Category:Schools and Research Institutions of the Edo Shogunate]] [[Category:Shibukawa family]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaji Clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442660]] Wikidata name: Tenmonkata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561574}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 入間川重恒|1=Irumagawa Shigetsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389078}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Noriyasu|1=Shibukawa Noriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561560}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|1=Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561556}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masakiyo|1=Shibukawa Masakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561581}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keichoku|1=Shibukawa Hironao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561576}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Nishikawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo}}.

After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|en|Inō Tadataka|WD=Q1240937|lt=Inō Tadataka}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsukuri Shūkichi|1=Mitsukuri Shūhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532071}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|en|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|WD=Q1327713|lt=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}} {{div col end}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tenmongata]][[ja:天文方]][[ko:천문방]][[th:เท็มมงกาตะ]][[zh:天文方]]
{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai|1=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar|1=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar|1=Xuanming calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō|1=Jisha-bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori|1=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Q17208865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken|1=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki|1=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies|1=Bansho Shirabesho}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai|1=Shibukawa Shunkai}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Hisatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561578}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiin|ja|渋川敬尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiin|1=Shibukawa Hirotada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561574}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiya|ja|渋川敬也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 入間川重恒|1=Irumagawa Shigetsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389078}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Noriyasu|ja|渋川則休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Noriyasu|1=Shibukawa Noriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561560}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|ja|渋川光洪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|1=Shibukawa Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561556}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masakiyo|ja|渋川正清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masakiyo|1=Shibukawa Masakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561581}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Masayō|ja|渋川正陽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Masayō|1=Shibukawa Masateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561582}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Keichoku|ja|渋川敬直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keichoku|1=Shibukawa Hironao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561576}}) – {{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō|1=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi|1=Yamaji Nushizumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi|1=Yamaji Nushizumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai|1=Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Related Articles == {{div col}} * {{ill|Inō Tadataka|en|Inō Tadataka|WD=Q1240937|lt=Inō Tadataka|1=Inō Tadataka}} * {{ill|Mitsukuri Shūkichi|ja|箕作秋坪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsukuri Shūkichi|1=Mitsukuri Shūhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532071}} * {{ill|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|en|National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|WD=Q1327713|lt=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan|1=National Astronomical Observatory of Japan}} {{div col end}}

{{Citation needed|date=July 2020}} '''Tenmonkata''' (天文方) was the astronomical and calendrical research institution established by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]]. Its primary responsibility was overseeing the creation of calendars ({{ill|Henreki|ja|編暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henreki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
== Overview ==
Initially, calendar creation was the responsibility of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}, a bureau under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} and supervised by the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. However, in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} (1684), {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai|1=Shibukawa Shunkai}} created the {{ill|Jōkyō calendar|en|Jōkyō calendar|WD=Q2353004|lt=Jōkyō calendar|1=Jōkyō calendar}}, replacing the traditional {{ill|Xuanming calendar|en|Xuanming calendar|WD=Q2511456|lt=Senmyō calendar|1=Xuanming calendar}}. Following this achievement, the Shogunate established the Tenmonkata under the supervision of the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|WD=Q1036279|lt=Jisha Bugyō|1=Jisha-bugyō}}. On {{ill|December 1, 1684|ja|貞享元年12月1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 1, 1684|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 5, 1685), Shunkai was appointed the first Tenmonkata, which also became the name of the role. Calendar-making responsibilities shifted from the Imperial Court to the Shogunate, and the Tenmonkata carried out its tasks under Shogunate authority. Initially under the Jisha Bugyō, it was placed under the {{ill|Wakadoshiyori|en|Wakadoshiyori|WD=Q1036547|lt=Wakadoshiyori|1=Wakadoshiyori}} on {{ill|January 23, 1747|ja|延享4th year 1月23日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 23, 1747|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The position was hereditary, but capable individuals with expertise in astronomy were occasionally appointed through adoption or special selection. By the end of the Edo period, eight families held the position: the {{ill|Shibukawa family|ja|渋川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inokai family|ja|猪飼家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishikawa family|ja|西川家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa family|1=Q17208865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208865}}, {{ill|Yamaji family|ja|山路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|lt=Yoshida family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida family|qq=}}, {{ill|Okumura family|ja|奥村家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumura family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥村氏}}, {{ill|Takahashi family|ja|高橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Adachi family|ja|足立家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some families, such as those of {{ill|Nishikawa Joken|en|Nishikawa Joken|WD=Q2753679|lt=Nishikawa Joken|1=Nishikawa Joken}} and {{ill|Takahashi Yoshitoki|en|Takahashi Yoshitoki|WD=Q11671571|lt=Takahashi Yoshitoki|1=Takahashi Yoshitoki}}, did not maintain their roles until the end of the period. By the late Edo period, only the Shibukawa, Yamaji, and Adachi families continued in the position.
In the 8th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} (1811), {{ill|Takahashi Kageyasu|ja|高橋景保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi Kageyasu|1=Takahashi Kageyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11671324}} proposed establishing the {{ill|Bansho Wage Goyo|ja|蛮書和解御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansho Wage Goyo|1=Banshowagegyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47482896}}, which lasted until the establishment of the {{ill|Bansho Shirabesho|en|Bansho Shirabesho|WD=Q2883486|lt=Institute for Western Studies|1=Bansho Shirabesho}} in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3 (1858). By the late Edo period, the Tenmonkata also handled astronomical observations, surveying, geography, and the translation of Western books, serving as one of the predecessors of {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=The University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}.
== The Tenmonkata Families ==
; Shibukawa Family : Owing to Shibukawa Shunkai's achievements, his family assumed the role of Tenmonkata in Jōkyō 1 (1684) and maintained the position, despite several adoptions, until the late Edo period. : {{ill|Shibukawa Shunkai|en|Shibukawa Shunkai|WD=Q786877|lt=Shibukawa Shunkai|1=Shibukawa Shunkai}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Mukai|ja|渋川昔尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Mukai|1=Shibukawa Keiyū|ja|渋川景佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiyū|1=Shibukawa Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561577}} – ({{ill|Shibukawa Sukemasa|ja|渋川佑賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Sukemasa|1=Shibukawa Sukekata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561557}} – {{ill|Shibukawa Keiten|ja|渋川敬典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa Keiten|1=Yoshinori Shibukawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561575}}
; Inokai Family : {{ill|Inokai Masakazu|ja|猪飼正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokai Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716). After his death in {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|WD=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō|1=Kanpō}} 1 (1741), the family ceased its role.
; Nishikawa Family : Nishikawa Joken’s son, {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}}, was appointed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|WD=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 4 (1747). The position lasted for two generations. : {{ill|Nishikawa Masayasu|ja|西川正休|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Masayasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西川如見|1=Nishikawa Joken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2753679}} – {{ill|Nishikawa Tadataka|ja|西川忠喬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Tadataka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Yamaji Family : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi|1=Yamaji Nushizumi}} became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). His descendants continued until the end of the Edo period. : {{ill|Yamaji Nushizumi|en|Yamaji Nushizumi|WD=Q11470954|lt=Yamaji Sukezumi|1=Yamaji Nushizumi}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Yukiyuki|ja|山路之徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Yukiyuki|1=Yamaji Yukiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532196}}) – {{ill|Yamaji Tokufū|ja|山路徳風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Tokufū|1=Yamaji Yoshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470962}} – {{ill|Yamaji Kanetaka|ja|山路諧孝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kanetaka|1=Yamaji Yukitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6532207}} – {{ill|Yamaji Akitsune|ja|山路彰常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akitsune|1=Yamaji Akitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470960}} – ({{ill|Yamaji Akizumi|ja|山路彰善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Akizumi|1=Yamaji Akiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470961}})
; Yoshida Family : {{ill|Sasaki Nagahide|ja|佐々木長秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, later {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became Tenmonkata in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). : {{ill|Yoshida Nagahide|ja|吉田秀長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Nagahide|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Yoshida Hideaki|ja|吉田秀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Hideaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Observatories == The first observatory, '''Sitendai''', was established in {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 2 (1685) in {{ill|Ushigome Wakamachi|ja|牛込藁町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigome Wakamachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It moved to {{ill|Surugadai|en|Surugadai|WD=Q3276871|lt=Kanda Surugadai|1=Surugadai}} in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 14 (1701) and later to {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|WD=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 13 (1842), a second observatory was established in {{ill|Kudanzaka|ja|九段坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudanzaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九段|1=Kudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370414}}. Both were abolished in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 (1869).
== Abolition of the Tenmonkata == After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, responsibility for calendrical work returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3 (1870), calendrical duties were transferred to the Ministry of Education's {{ill|Astronomical Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
